what_it_does: Records a set of lens-specific, non-obvious design catches for MAP3, organized under seven perspectives (iOS, Voice, Pro Annotator, Graph Architect, Skeptic, Annotation-tools, Minimalist), as a noticeboard block.
observations: Frontmatter identifies the item as a current block sourced from claude_code in the dragnet-development channel, linked as part_of board://item-1deff1b2.
Body lists seven single-lens catches: iOS web-haptics and standalone-PWA edge-swipe conflicts as device-verify-only risks; an ARC menu in a configurable bottom thumb-zone; named precedents.
Voice lens emphasizes dictation correction (tap-to-respeak / candidate chips), TTS replies, and a dual-purpose 'V' push-to-talk plus command-discovery surface.
Pro Annotator lens defines the review pass as the unit of work, with stet / reopen / who-may-resolve thread semantics and inline-diff vs margin-comment registers.

Graph Architect lens warns that path-structured addresses would violate the flat-opaque board:// identity law and orphan annotations on edits; node-identity-across-reparse is the crux; envelope maps to concrete schemes (frame, @v, cas://, ui://→code://, item_types).
Skeptic lens flags a 'granularity lie': atoms exist at file/coarse level but the feature needs element/sub-paragraph level, with many things depending on an unbuilt tree.
Annotation-tools lens cites famous tools that orphan comments and why, and frames comment-vs-suggestion as the proven loop.
Minimalist lens rejects 'generality as the deliverable' but the closing prescription is cut off.
standouts: Voice dictation correction is called make-or-break for the experience.
Graph Architect states that path-structured addresses would violate the existing board:// identity law and orphan annotations on any edit.
Skeptic identifies a hidden dependency on an unbuilt element/sub-paragraph tree.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter, id/address/type/source/state/title/author_session/channel/links/created/updated/history.
Uses all-caps lens names as implicit section markers inside a single bullet list.
Telegraphic, parenthetical design notation mixing risk, precedent, prescription, and implementation hint.
Markdown body is a heading plus bullets, no subheadings or numbered sections.
concerns: The Minimalist bullet is incomplete: 'the working loop should be.'
Claims about famous tools, schemes, and the unbuilt tree lack identifiers or links inside this file.
Bullets blend observation, risk, and prescription without explicit priority, owner, or verification status.
No explicit decision or action traceability for each catch.
notes: A lens-indexed capture of subtle MAP3 design risks, with the board:// identity constraint and the unbuilt-tree granularity warning as the most concrete hidden blockers.
questions: What is MAP3 and which deliverable or subsystem do these lenses apply to?
Which specific annotation tools are described as orphaning comments, and why?
What is the intended 'working loop' the Minimalist lens is advocating?
How exactly do the envelope schemes (frame, @v, cas://, ui://→code://, item_types) map to implementation?
Who owns verification of the iOS Safari/PWA edge-swipe and web-haptics risks?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title and body both collect non-obvious, high-value catches that each lens saw individually.
apparent_themes: Multi-perspective design review via named lenses
Granularity mismatch as a hidden dependency risk
Voice interaction correctness and correction loops
Annotation threading, resolution, and suggestion semantics
Minimalism versus generality in product framing
intention_signals: To surface high-signal, orthogonal design risks and constraints early so they can be addressed before they become implementation blockers.
novelty: The use of named expert 'lenses' to index a single noticeboard item of orthogonal, non-obvious design catches.
contribution: A compact, lens-indexed inventory of subtle MAP3 design constraints and risks, including the board:// identity-law violation risk, the voice dictation-correction loop, review-pass semantics, and the granularity-lie warning.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item titled 'MAP3 · What only one lens saw' that records seven lens-specific non-obvious design catches for a code/design substrate, covering iOS haptics/gestures, voice dictation correction, annotation review passes, graph identity laws, granularity assumptions, annotation tool failures, and minimalist framing.
intention_for_embedding: To capture unique high-value observations from distinct expert lenses so they inform design decisions and avoid hidden risks.
